title: The Tower That Forgot
intro: A trader from the Bastion swaps you a Story for a sack of grain: the tale of the White wizard Serevane, who bound a fortress to walk on stone legs and carry her people out of the Fracture's path. It walked for a year and a day. Then, one still morning, it forgot how a wall holds up a roof — and came down all at once, with everyone she had meant to save still inside.

advisors.text: Order is the first thing the Forgetting takes, Archon. Serevane's fortress did not break — it lost the memory of being whole. As may we all.
One story, told for grain. It may be true; it may be a Bastion's bad dream dressed as history. Weigh it — don't kneel to it.

choices.label: Record the Story in the coven's own book

win.text: Serevane's walking fortress joins your slow-growing library of the ways magic has failed. A grim shelf — and the truest map you own of the road the whole world is walking.
win.saga: You traded grain for the Story of Serevane, whose walking fortress forgot how a wall holds a roof and fell — and set it in the coven's book of magic's failures.

choices.label: Trade the Story onward for coin

win.text: A grim tale sells as well as a glad one. You pass Serevane's fall along the market roads and pocket the difference.
win.saga: You took the Story of Serevane's fallen walking fortress and traded it onward for coin — a grim tale sells as well as a glad one.

lose.text: No one wants to buy an omen this season. The Story stays with you, heavier for having tried to sell it.
lose.saga: You tried to sell on the Story of Serevane's fallen fortress, but no one wanted an omen that season, and it stayed with you, heavier.
